title: China refocuses on financial risk – and ramps up NPL disposals
dek: China's regulators are pushing faster bad-loan cleanups, even as banks balance capital needs against NPL recognition and write-offs.
keyFindings: Chinese banks have disposed of around RMB 3 trillion in NPLs annually since 2020, but disposal intensity relative to total loans has declined.
Falling net interest margins and capital constraints likely encouraged slower NPL recognition to preserve profits and replenish capital.
Regulatory pressure to increase NPL disposals in 2024 could create new secondary-market supply, especially if banks receive recapitalization support.

paragraphs: China's banks have disposed of a record amount of nonperforming loans (NPLs) over the past four years, and authorities are signaling that disposals should accelerate in 2024.

The People's Bank of China (PBoC) has said writing off NPLs is part of its campaign to revitalize inefficient credit, while the National Financial Regulatory Administration (NFRA) has prioritized higher NPL disposals.

Although annual disposals have remained large in absolute terms, bank lending expanded sharply between 2020 and 2023, reducing disposal intensity relative to outstanding loans and suggesting hidden stress may have accumulated on balance sheets.

sections.heading: Prioritizing Capital Over NPL Recognition
paragraphs: Chinese banks must maintain minimum risk-adjusted capital as loan books grow, but many have had limited ability to raise private equity capital at attractive valuations.
At the same time, lower lending rates have compressed net interest margins, making retained earnings more important for capital replenishment.
Recognizing fewer NPLs can temporarily support profits by reducing required provisioning expenses, helping banks preserve core capital while delaying full balance-sheet cleanup.

sections.heading: Policy Trade-Offs and Market Implications
paragraphs: If regulators permit lower provisioning buffers at stronger banks, write-offs could increase without an immediate hit to reported profitability.
However, provisioning capacity varies across institutions; smaller regional banks may require explicit state support to raise disposal volumes materially.
For distressed-debt investors, a policy refocus on financial risk and NPL resolution can expand the opportunity set as banks and AMCs seek larger offloads.
